# Jonggrang Progress Log — cli
# Created: 2026-06-09

## task-001 — Infrastructure setup (test/ dir, scripts, helpers)
- Added test/helpers.js (CommonJS, matches src/ style; zero deps, node: builtins only):
  captureOutput() (patches stdout/stderr write + console.log/error, getters + restore()),
  tempConfigHome() (mkdtempSync under os.tmpdir, sets XDG_CONFIG_HOME, cleanup restores env + rm -rf),
  startServer() (http on port 0 / 127.0.0.1, returns {url,port,server,close}, default 200 "{}").
- Added npm scripts: "test": "node --test", "test:coverage": "node --test --experimental-test-coverage".
- GOTCHA: `node --test` runs EVERY file under test/, including helpers.js, counting it as 1
  passing no-op "test". Harmless (helpers only define fns), but future helpers must stay side-effect-free.
  Exit code is 0 with no real tests yet — acceptance met.
- tempConfigHome MUST be called BEFORE require('../src/config') — config.js computes its path at load time.

## task-002 — Export parseFlags from src/cli.js
- Changed `module.exports = { run };` → `module.exports = { run, parseFlags };` (one-line additive).
- ensureKey/interactive/process.exit/stdin paths left private and untouched — only parseFlags surfaced.
- Verified: require('../src/cli').parseFlags is a function; parseFlags(['list','--json','--page','2'])
  returns {flags:{json:true,page:"2"},positional:["list"]}. `node --test` still 0 fail.

## task-003 — Util tests (checkResp, readData, maybeJson)
- Added test/util.test.js: 19 tests, describe/test blocks, node:assert/strict. Full suite now 20.
- IMPORTANT: task description mis-described readData/maybeJson (claimed JSON-parse/raw-fallback
  semantics). Tested ACTUAL src/util.js behavior instead:
  * checkResp(res): 2xx → returns undefined; else throws Error w/ .status + .body. Message =
    `API ${status} — ${msg}`, msg = body.messages || body.message || res.raw || ''. Boundary
    cases covered (199 throws, 200/299 pass, 300 throws).
  * readData(value): falsy → undefined; '@'-prefix → fs.readFileSync + JSON.parse; else JSON.parse.
    @file branch tested with a REAL temp file (mkdtemp/writeFile/rm in finally) — mock-free.
  * maybeJson(flags, body, fallback): flags.json → ui.jsonOut(body) (writes JSON+\n to stdout),
    return true; else fn fallback → call+true; else false. Non-function fallback → false.
- maybeJson writes to stdout via ui.jsonOut; captured (not stubbed) with helpers.captureOutput()
  to both assert output and keep test logs clean. cap.restore() in finally.
- The skill 'library/testing/unit-testing-patterns' has NO SKILL.md on disk — followed existing
  helpers.js conventions instead.

## task-004 — CLI parse tests (parseFlags)
- Added test/cli.test.js: 20 tests across 6 describe blocks; full suite now 43. parseFlags is
  pure (no I/O, no process.exit), so each case is a direct call + deepEqual on {flags, positional}.
  No helpers needed.
- IMPORTANT: task desc said "short flags (-f value)" but src/cli.js has NO generic short-flag
  handling. Only short flags are -h/-v (booleans). An unknown short token like '-f' doesn't start
  with '--', so it falls through to the positional branch — '-f' AND its 'value' both become
  positionals. Tested ACTUAL behavior (covered -h/-v booleans + the -f fall-through case).
- Covered each documented style: --flag value (apiKey/page/pageSize/page-size alias/data + unknown
  long flag consuming next token), --key=value (incl. '=' in value, empty value), boolean (--json
  /--force), short (-h/-v), positionals (incl. '--' terminator pushing remainder verbatim), and
  mixed-order combos (incl. last-wins overwrite, missing trailing value → undefined).

## task-005 — Config round-trip tests (temp XDG_CONFIG_HOME)
- Added test/config.test.js: 12 tests across 3 describe blocks; full suite now 55.
- CRITICAL gotcha: src/config.js freezes BOTH paths at module load:
  * XDG path from XDG_CONFIG_HOME (line 5) — isolated via helpers.tempConfigHome().
  * legacy path from os.homedir() i.e. $HOME (line 9) — NOT covered by tempConfigHome.
    To safely test the legacy ~/.mayar migration without touching the real home,
    ALSO set process.env.HOME to a second temp dir BEFORE require('../src/config').
    (os.homedir() honors $HOME on POSIX.) Restore/rm both in an after() hook.
- Covered: path-location sanity (file/dir under temp XDG), load null when empty,
  save→load round-trip, on-disk persistence, overwrite, pretty 2-space JSON,
  clear→true+removes / clear→false when absent, malformed JSON → load() null,
  legacy migration (copies to XDG, removes legacyFile+legacyDir, returns data),
  XDG-precedence (no migration when XDG file exists), null when neither exists.
- node --test spawns a separate child process per test file, so the global
  HOME/XDG env mutation in config.test.js does not leak into other test files.
- Verified post-run: no /root/.config/mayar and no /root/.mayar created.

## task-006 — UI tests (table() + color wrappers, non-TTY)
- Added test/ui.test.js: 15 tests across 4 describe blocks; full suite now 70, 0 fail.
- KEY: src/ui.js freezes `isTTY = !!process.stdout.isTTY` at MODULE LOAD (line 3), so
  behavior is fixed the instant you require('../src/ui'). node --test runs non-TTY
  (process.stdout.isTTY === undefined) → wrappers return String(s) plain, no ANSI.
- Robustness: gate the non-TTY assertions with `const skip = NON_TTY ? false : 'msg'`
  passed as node:test's per-test `{ skip }` option, so the suite stays green even if
  ever run under a TTY. captureOutput() patches .write, NOT .isTTY, so the frozen
  value (and thus plain output) is unaffected by capturing.
- Color wrappers (dim/bold/red/green/yellow/cyan/magenta) coerce via String(): red(42)
  ==='42', dim(null)==='null', etc. Asserted no /\x1b\[/ in any output.
- table() layout math (verified exactly): width = min(48, max(colNameLen, ...cellLens));
  columns joined with a 2-space gutter; cells padEnd(width). Empty/null/undefined rows
  → dim('(no rows)')+'\n' (plain in non-TTY). Cells use `r[col] ?? ''` so 0 is kept
  but null/undefined/missing → ''. Values longer than width → slice(0,width-1)+'…';
  width cap 48 means a 50-char value renders as 47×'x'+'…' (48 display chars).

## task-007 — Wire-up pre-commit hook + document coverage reality
- pre_commit hook (.jonggrang/jonggrang.json) changed from "echo 'no test command
  configured'" → "node --test". The full suite (70 tests, 0 fail) now gates every commit.
  Used bare `node --test` (not `npm test`) to match the task spec and avoid an npm subshell;
  it picks up every test/*.test.js the same way the "test" npm script does.
- NOTE: both files this task touches live under .jonggrang/ (jonggrang state), which is
  intentionally kept OUT of the feature branch (recent commits only carry test/ + src/cli.js).
  So this task produces NO git commit — the changes are state-only and stay in the working tree.

### Coverage reality (the 80% threshold gap)
- testing.coverage_threshold in jonggrang.json is 80, and this first pass DOES NOT meet it.
  This is intentional, not a miss. The suite deliberately scopes to pure / near-pure functions
  that need no mocking: util.* (checkResp/readData/maybeJson), cli.parseFlags, config round-trip
  (temp XDG_CONFIG_HOME + HOME), and ui.table()/color wrappers (non-TTY). These are the parts
  that can be tested mock-free with high signal and zero flakiness.
- UNCOVERED by design: src/commands/* (each command wires api + ui together), the interactive /
  process.exit paths (ensureKey, ui.ask, ui.askSecret), the HTTP layer in src/api.js, and the
  top-level run() dispatch in src/cli.js. Covering these honestly requires either dependency
  seams the CommonJS code doesn't have yet, or a live transport — both deferred (see below).
- Decision rationale: forcing 80% now would mean brittle require.cache surgery / monkey-patching
  to fake api + ui, producing tests that assert "the mock was called" rather than real behavior —
  false confidence + a large maintenance surface. We chose honest, durable coverage over a
  green threshold number. The threshold stays at 80 as the documented target for the next layers.

### Follow-up layers (to close the gap, in rough ROI order)
1. Command-level tests with mocking: introduce a dependency seam (e.g. export commands as
   factories taking {api, ui}, or a small injectable module registry) so src/commands/* can run
   against fake api/ui. This is the biggest coverage lever; it needs a (behavior-preserving)
   refactor for DI, so it was kept out of this pass's files array.
2. Local HTTP integration tests: use test/helpers.js startServer() (already built — http on
   port 0, returns {url,port,close}) with MAYAR_API_URL/BASE_URL pointed at it (set BEFORE
   require, since api.js freezes BASE_URL at load) to exercise checkResp + the real fetch path
   end-to-end without external network. Slower/flakier than unit tests, so a second layer, not
   the foundation.
3. Interactive-path coverage: ensureKey / ui.ask / ui.askSecret call process.exit and drive raw
   stdin; testing them needs either extraction of the testable core or a child-process harness
   that feeds stdin and asserts exit codes. Lowest ROI, do last.

## task-001 (login-env-and-skill-install) — Add pickFromList() to src/ui.js
- Added async pickFromList(items, { displayKey, descriptionKey }) to src/ui.js.
- Renders numbered list (1-indexed) with display values from displayKey; optional
  descriptionKey renders descriptions indented 3 spaces in dim below each item.
- Empty items array: prints dim('(no items)') and returns null immediately.
- Prompt loop: calls ask('Pick a number (or q to quit): ') — same readline pattern as
  existing ask(). On 'q'/'Q' → null. On numeric input: parseInt trimmed string, reject
  NaN or partial-match ("12abc") with dim message, reject out-of-range (1-N). Returns
  the selected item on valid input.
- PATTERN: displayKey lookup uses `item[displayKey] ?? ''` to safely handle missing keys
  (same ?? pattern as table()'s cell access).
- PATTERN: error messages use dim() for visual consistency with the existing ui style.
- GOTCHA: pickFromList is async and interacts with stdin, making it harder to test
  without mocking ask() or feeding stdin. Not yet covered by the test suite — fits
  the "interactive-path coverage" bucket in the follow-up layers above.
- Full test suite still passes: 70 tests, 0 fail.
- Added to module.exports alongside existing exports (printBanner, ask, askSecret,
  jsonOut, table, and color wrappers).

## task-002 — Create docs command module: llms.txt fetch, parse, and cache infrastructure
- Created src/commands/docs.js with 7 infrastructure functions + run() entry point.
- Functions implemented:
  * getCacheDir() — returns config.dir/cache, creates with mkdirSync(mode 0o700).
  * getCachedLLMsTxt() — reads llms.txt + llms-meta.json, checks 24h TTL, returns text or null.
  * readStaleCache() (internal) — reads cached llms.txt ignoring TTL for stale-fallback.
  * fetchLLMsTxt() — https.get with 10s timeout (req.setTimeout), follows 301/302 up to 3 hops,
    User-Agent: mayar-cli/<version>, writes cache files on success, throws on failure.
  * fetchOrGetLLMsTxt(refresh) — orchestrates cache/fetch; refresh=true skips cache entirely;
    on fetch failure: stale cache → dim warning + return stale; no cache → throw with
    "Visit https://docs.mayar.id for manual browsing" message.
  * parseTitleUrl(line) — regex /^-\s+\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/ extracts {title, url} or null.
  * slugify(title) — lowercase, replace [^a-z0-9]+ with '-', trim leading/trailing dashes.
  * parseLLMsTxt(raw) — splits by newline, identifies ## sections, parses list items into
    {sections: [{name, topics: [{slug, title, url, description, section}]}]}, skips empty sections.
  * run(ctx) — CLI entry for mayar docs: fetches+parses llms.txt, supports --refresh, --json,
    keyword topic search (case-insensitive matching title/slug/description), TTY interactive
    pickFromList, non-TTY plain listing. No API key required (handled as special case in cli.js).
- Updated src/cli.js:
  * Added --refresh boolean flag to parseFlags().
  * Added docs command routing before ensureKey() block (like init/login, no auth needed).
  * Added "Documentation" section to HELP() text with docs [topic] [--json] [--refresh].
- All 70 existing tests pass (0 fail).
- GOTCHA: docs command intentionally bypasses ensureKey() — documentation browsing is public
  and should not require authentication. Added as a special case alongside init/login in cli.js.
- PATTERN: fetch functions follow the same redirect-following pattern as skill.js (doFetch
  recursion, redirect counter, URL resolution via new URL(relative, base).href). Timeout uses
  req.setTimeout() (Node 18+ compatible) rather than options.timeout for broader compat.
- PATTERN: cache writes are wrapped in try/catch — non-fatal, data is still returned to caller
  even if cache dir is unwritable (e.g., permissions).

## task-003 — Add content fetcher and HTML renderer to docs command
- Added fetchPageContent(url) to src/commands/docs.js:
  * Uses https.get with User-Agent 'mayar-cli/<version>' header.
  * 10-second timeout via req.setTimeout(); on timeout, req.destroy() + reject with
    'Request timed out'.
  * Follows redirects (301/302/307/308) via Location header, up to 3 hops; throws
    'Too many redirects' on limit.
  * Resolves with UTF-8 body on any 2xx; rejects with 'HTTP <code>' on non-2xx.
  * Same doFetch recursion pattern as fetchLLMsTxt, but accepts 2xx range instead
    of just 200, and handles 307/308 in addition to 301/302.
- Added renderContent(html) to src/commands/docs.js:
  * (a) Extraction: tries <main>, then <article>, then <div class="*content*">,
    then <body>, then raw html — all via regex + case-insensitive flags.
  * (b) Tag stripping: replace(/<[^>]*>/g, '').
  * (c) Entity decoding: single-pass replace with callback — decodes &amp; &lt;
    &gt; &quot; &#39; &nbsp; &#x2F;. Single-pass avoids ordering issues (e.g.,
    &amp;lt; → &lt; → < if done sequentially).
  * (d) Collapses 3+ consecutive newlines into 2.
  * (e) Trims leading/trailing whitespace per line.
  * (f) Terminal formatting: markdown headings (^#{1,6}\s) → ui.bold(); code
    fences (```) toggle indentation, content inside gets 2-space prefix; horizontal
    rules (--- or *** exactly) → ui.dim('─'×40). Returns formatted string — does
    NOT print directly (caller decides output).
- PATTERN: entity decoding uses a single regex with a map callback to avoid the
  &amp; → & → re-decode pitfall that would occur with chained .replace() calls.
- PATTERN: renderContent is pure (string in, string out) — no I/O, no process writes.
  Makes it trivially testable without mocking.
- Both functions added to module.exports alongside existing exports.

## task-004 — Implement docs command UX: interactive, keyword, non-TTY, and topic resolution
- Rewrote the run() function with 6 distinct UX paths:
  * (1) Fetch+parse flow: build flat allTopics[] from all sections' topics for unified lookup.
  * (2) Topic resolution (keyword given): slugify(input) → filter where inputSlug is substring
    of topic slug OR input is case-insensitive substring of topic title (description excluded).
  * (3a) Single match: fetchPageContent(url), renderContent(html), print header in format
    ui.bold(title) + ' — ' + ui.cyan(url), print rendered content, footer ui.dim('───').
    fetch failures are caught gracefully with dim('could not fetch page content').
  * (3b) Multiple matches: numbered list (1-indexed) with title, section in dim, and
    description indented on next line.
  * (3c) No match: 'No documentation found for "<input>"' + fuzzy 'Did you mean?' suggestion.
    similarityScore(input, title): prefix match bonus (+100) + ordered shared character count.
    Takes top 3 by score (filtering out zero-scorers).
  * (4) No-arg TTY: section headers as ui.bold(sec.name), then pickFromList(allTopics, ...).
    If selected → fetchPageContent + renderContent; if null (quit) → exit silently.
  * (5) No-arg non-TTY: '## SectionName' headers, '  - Title — description' entries.
  * (6) JSON mode: minimal stub preserved for task-006 to enhance.
- Added similarityScore() function — exported alongside existing exports.
- PATTERN: topic resolution uses slugify() on the input keyword and checks slug.includes()
  rather than direct contains on the raw input, which makes 'create-invoice' match topics
  like 'create-invoice-link' even if the user typed 'create-invoice'.
- PATTERN: all output uses process.stdout.write (not console.log) per task spec.
- GOTCHA: the similarityScore shared-character-count uses ordered matching (characters from
  input appearing in sequence in the title), not a simple character-set intersection. This
  gives 'invoic' a score of 6 against 'Create Invoice' (i-n-v-o-i-c found in order) and
  'xyz' a score of 0. This is more robust than a simple intersection for typo detection.
- Full test suite: 70 tests, 0 fail (unchanged — interactive/network paths untested by design).

## task-005 — Register docs command in CLI, add help text, and parse --refresh flag
- Most of the three changes described in the task were already done by task-002:
  * parseFlags() already had `else if (a === '--refresh') flags.refresh = true;`
  * HELP() already had the Documentation section with `docs [topic] [--json] [--refresh]`
  * run() already had `if (cmd === 'docs')` explicit routing before ensureKey()
- Two items remained: (a) `--refresh` was missing from the Global flags section of HELP();
  (b) `docs` was missing from the handlers map (the explicit `if` check handled it instead).
- Added `--refresh` flag description after `--data` line in the Global flags section.
- Added `docs: './commands/docs'` to handlers map (alphabetically after `discounts`).
  This is technically unreachable for docs (the explicit `if` catches it first), but serves
  as documentation/completeness. If future refactoring normalizes all commands through the
  handlers map, the entry is already in place.
- GOTCHA: task description was written before task-002 implemented these changes, so
  task-005 became a delta rather than a full implementation. Always re-read the current
  source before starting a task — don't assume the preconditions from the task description
  still hold.
- Full test suite: 70 tests, 0 fail (no new tests — this was a wiring-only change).

## task-006 — Implement --json output mode for docs command
- Replaced the minimal JSON stub in run() with full four-case implementation:
  * (1) No topic: ui.jsonOut({ topics: [{ slug, title, url, description, section }] }), return.
  * (2) Single match: fetchPageContent + renderContent, ui.jsonOut({ topic, title, url, content }), return.
  * (3) Multi-match: ui.jsonOut({ matches: [{ topic: slug, title, url, description }] }), return — no prompt.
  * (4) No match: ui.jsonOut({ error: 'No documentation found for "<input>"', suggestions: [top3Slugs] }),
    process.exitCode = 1, return — no process.exit().
- Fixed stale cache warning to use process.stderr.write instead of process.stdout.write,
  so it doesn't pollute JSON stdout output.
- Fixed outer fetch error handler: JSON mode now sets process.exitCode = 1 and returns
  instead of calling process.exit(1), which would hide the JSON error output.
- JSON path topic matching uses the same logic as the non-JSON path: slugify(input) →
  slug.includes() + title.toLowerCase().includes().
- Zero-match suggestions use similarityScore() (same fuzzy matching as non-JSON) and
  emit only the slug strings (not full objects).
- Page fetch failures in single-match JSON path are caught and output as ui.jsonOut
  error with exitCode = 1.
- PATTERN: JSON mode short-circuits ALL interactive and plain-text output paths — only
  ui.jsonOut() writes to stdout. All four cases end with an explicit return.
- Full test suite: 70 tests, 0 fail (no new tests — JSON path is network-dependent).
